items.title: Administrative identities require MFA and conditional access in the scoped production environment.
items.rationale: Deterministic machine state over a narrow identity predicate.

items.title: Infrastructure access in the scoped production environment requires unique named identities with no shared administrative accounts.
items.rationale: Deterministic identity-state evidence over a narrow unique-authentication predicate.

items.title: Scoped customer and environment boundaries are enforced with no undeclared cross-environment paths in the production slice.
items.rationale: Deterministic architecture-state evidence over a narrow tenant and environment segmentation predicate.

items.title: Scoped password-quality controls enforce strong minimum length, no maximum-length restriction, and common-password blocking.
items.rationale: Deterministic identity-policy evidence over a narrow password-quality predicate.

items.title: A managed web application firewall is attached to the scoped public ingress path, runs in blocking mode, and has a managed rule set active.
items.rationale: Deterministic perimeter-state evidence over a narrow managed-WAF predicate.

items.title: Audit logging is enabled with a declared retention window for the scoped production runtime.
items.rationale: Deterministic machine state over a narrow logging predicate.

items.title: Scoped runtime telemetry is forwarded to the declared central monitoring sink with detection rules enabled.
items.rationale: Deterministic telemetry-state evidence over a narrow centralized-monitoring predicate.

items.title: Public ingress is HTTPS-only with managed certificates and TLS 1.2 or higher.
items.rationale: Deterministic edge configuration over a narrow TLS predicate.

items.title: The managed network boundary is attached to the declared ingress path for scoped public services.
items.rationale: Deterministic ingress-path attachment state over a narrow managed-boundary predicate.

items.title: Administrative ingress is restricted to approved source ranges at the managed network boundary.
items.rationale: Deterministic ingress-rule state over a narrow approved-source restriction predicate.

items.title: Plaintext transport is disabled on the scoped public ingress path.
items.rationale: Deterministic transport-policy state over a narrow plaintext-disabled predicate.

items.title: Encryption at rest is enabled for the scoped customer data stores and no unencrypted store is present.
items.rationale: Deterministic storage-configuration state over a narrow encryption-at-rest predicate.

items.title: Scoped runtime service accounts do not use user-managed long-lived keys.
items.rationale: Deterministic machine credential state over a narrow key-hygiene predicate.

items.title: The scoped workload runs only in the declared approved regions.
items.rationale: Deterministic deployment-boundary state over a narrow location predicate.

items.title: Backup snapshots are scheduled with a declared immutable retention window.
items.rationale: Deterministic backup configuration over a narrow recovery predicate.

items.title: Security awareness completion is documented for the current review period.
items.rationale: Documentary training evidence is attestation-routed in the current corridor.

items.title: A restore exercise is documented inside the declared review window.
items.rationale: Recovery exercise evidence is attestation-routed in the current corridor.

items.title: The processor register is current and DPA coverage is declared for the reviewed subprocessors.
items.rationale: Third-party and processor evidence is currently documentary rather than machine-proved.

items.title: The DSR runbook is approved and has a named owner for the current review window.
items.rationale: Privacy operations documentation is currently attestation-routed.

items.title: Privacy-by-design adequacy has been demonstrated for the full product change process.
items.rationale: Privacy-by-design adequacy remains explicitly outside the machine-proof corridor.

items.title: A typed export of periodic access review evidence is present for the current review window.
items.rationale: Periodic access review can be machine-checked only when a typed export exists.

items.title: Default branch protections are enforced for the scoped production repository.
items.rationale: Deterministic repository policy state over a narrow branch-protection predicate.

items.title: CI workflow policy constrains deployment to reviewed workflows and protected refs.
items.rationale: Deterministic delivery-policy state over a narrow CI workflow predicate.

items.title: The production change-review policy is approved and declares an emergency change path for the current review window.
items.rationale: The wider change-review governance layer is still documentary even when repo and CI guardrails are machine-checked.

items.title: Access review closure and high-risk exception resolution are attested for the current review window.
items.rationale: Access-review closure is an accountable signoff activity and remains attestation-routed even when the export slice is machine-checkable.

items.title: Monitoring review cadence, ownership, and retention operations are attested for the scoped production telemetry program.
items.rationale: Monitoring review operations remain documentary and owner-attested even when telemetry forwarding is machine-checkable.

items.title: Configuration exceptions are current, owned, and not already expired in the current review window.
items.rationale: Baseline exception handling is a documentary governance slice and should stay separate from machine-checked hardening state.

items.title: Patch exceptions declare compensating controls and no declared exception is already expired in the current review window.
items.rationale: Patch exception handling is documentary governance evidence rather than a runtime theorem.

items.title: Patch cadence and exception thresholds are adequate for the in-scope production assets.
items.rationale: Patch adequacy stays explicitly judgment-routed even when patch state and exception records are structured.

items.title: The incident response runbook is approved, owned, and current for the declared review window.
items.rationale: Incident procedure ownership and approval are documentary facts and should stay attestation-routed.

items.title: Incident escalation contacts and notification paths are current for the declared review window.
items.rationale: Escalation paths and responder contacts remain documentary governance evidence in the current corridor.

items.title: High-risk vendor agreements carry the required security and privacy terms for the current review window.
items.rationale: Contract-term presence is a documentary slice and should not be collapsed into a wider vendor-program claim.

items.title: Vendor tiering, review cadence, and inherited-control allocation are adequate for the in-scope dependency set.
items.rationale: Vendor-program adequacy stays explicitly judgment-routed even when narrower vendor facts are structured.

items.title: Data retention schedules and exception handling are documented and current for the scoped lifecycle program.
items.rationale: Retention schedules remain documentary governance evidence even when individual deletion events are outside the current proof corridor.

items.title: Retention periods, exception scope, and deletion adequacy are sufficient for the in-scope data lifecycle surface.
items.rationale: Retention and deletion adequacy stays explicitly judgment-routed even when narrower schedule attestations are structured.

items.title: Scoped service and supplier agreements carry the declared security commitment terms for the current review window.
items.rationale: Agreement-term presence is a documentary slice and should stay separate from the wider adequacy judgment about those commitments.

items.title: Ownership, review cadence, and exception handling are current for scoped security commitment governance.
items.rationale: Governance over supplier security commitments remains documentary and owner-attested in the current corridor.

items.title: The scoped security commitments and their governance are adequate for the supplier and transfer risks in scope.
items.rationale: Supplier security-commitment adequacy stays judgment-routed even when narrower terms and governance facts are structured.

items.title: Outsourced development relationships carry current security requirements for the declared review window.
items.rationale: Supplier-development requirement presence remains documentary governance evidence rather than a theorem about delivery behaviour.

items.title: Oversight and exception handling are current for scoped outsourced development relationships.
items.rationale: Outsourced-development oversight stays attestation-routed because the current public corridor captures governance records, not full supplier-performance proofs.

items.title: The outsourced development model is adequate for the scoped third-party delivery risks.
items.rationale: Outsourced-development adequacy stays explicitly judgment-routed even when requirement and oversight records are structured.

items.title: A current intake path exists for reported security concerns relevant to the scoped service operations.
items.rationale: Concern intake remains documentary governance evidence and should stay separate from broader adequacy judgments about handling quality.

items.title: Tracking, ownership, and resolution follow-up are current for the scoped security-concern process.
items.rationale: Follow-up handling for reported concerns remains documentary governance evidence in the current corridor.

items.title: The scoped handling model for reported security concerns is adequate to triage, resolve, and learn from the issues that matter.
items.rationale: Security-concern handling adequacy stays judgment-routed even when narrower intake and follow-up facts are structured.

items.title: Scoped remote access traverses the declared remote-access path with device posture controls bounded for the systems in scope.
items.rationale: Deterministic remote-access boundary state over a narrow declared-path and device-posture predicate.

items.title: A current remote-working procedure covers incident reporting for the scoped workforce and review window.
items.rationale: Remote-working procedure evidence remains documentary and owner-attested in the current corridor.

items.title: The scoped remote-working model is adequate for the device, workspace, and incident-reporting risks that matter.
items.rationale: Remote-working adequacy stays explicitly judgment-routed even when narrower posture and procedure facts are structured.

items.title: A current register of legal, regulatory, contractual, and compliance requirements is attested for the scoped ISMS surface.
items.rationale: Compliance-inventory presence remains documentary governance evidence in the current corridor.

items.title: Compliance requirement review cadence, ownership, and update handling are current for the scoped inventory.
items.rationale: Compliance-review governance remains documentary and owner-attested in the current corridor.

items.title: The scoped compliance inventory is adequate for the legal, regulatory, and contractual obligations that materially affect the ISMS.
items.rationale: Compliance-inventory adequacy stays explicitly judgment-routed even when narrower register and review facts are structured.

items.title: A current register of interested parties and stakeholder groups is attested for the scoped ISMS surface.
items.rationale: Stakeholder inventory evidence remains documentary governance evidence in the current corridor.

items.title: Stakeholder expectations, obligations, and review ownership are current for the scoped ISMS surface.
items.rationale: Stakeholder obligation evidence remains documentary and owner-attested in the current corridor.

items.title: The scoped stakeholder-management model is adequate for the interested parties and expectations that materially affect the ISMS.
items.rationale: Stakeholder-management adequacy stays judgment-routed even when narrower register and obligation facts are structured.

items.title: Current project-security requirements are defined for the project types in the scoped delivery portfolio.
items.rationale: Project-security requirements remain documentary governance evidence in the current corridor.

items.title: Project-security review and exception tracking are current for the scoped delivery portfolio.
items.rationale: Project-security review evidence remains documentary and owner-attested in the current corridor.

items.title: The scoped project-security model is adequate for integrating security into the project types and lifecycle stages that matter.
items.rationale: Project-security adequacy stays judgment-routed even when narrower requirements and review facts are structured.

items.title: Current customer support channels are documented and reachable for the scoped service surface.
items.rationale: Customer-support channel evidence remains documentary and owner-attested in the current corridor.

items.title: Support staffing, escalation ownership, and response governance are current for the scoped service surface.
items.rationale: Customer-support governance remains documentary and owner-attested in the current corridor.

items.title: The scoped customer-support model is adequate for the channel, escalation, and service risks that matter.
items.rationale: Customer-support adequacy stays judgment-routed even when narrower channel and governance facts are structured.

items.title: A current intellectual-property policy or requirement set is attested for the scoped service and workforce surface.
items.rationale: Intellectual-property policy evidence remains documentary governance evidence in the current corridor.

items.title: Issue tracking, handling rules, and ownership are current for scoped intellectual-property obligations.
items.rationale: Intellectual-property issue handling remains documentary and owner-attested in the current corridor.

items.title: The scoped intellectual-property posture is adequate to protect proprietary material and avoid misuse of third-party rights in scope.
items.rationale: Intellectual-property adequacy stays judgment-routed even when narrower policy and issue-handling facts are structured.

items.title: A current corrective-action process is defined with named ownership for the scoped ISMS improvement cycle.
items.rationale: Corrective-action process evidence remains documentary and owner-attested in the current corridor.

items.title: Corrective-action follow-up and remediation ownership are current for the scoped improvement cycle.
items.rationale: Corrective-action follow-up remains documentary and owner-attested in the current corridor.

items.title: The scoped continual-improvement model is adequate to identify, prioritize, and close the issues that matter.
items.rationale: Continual-improvement adequacy stays judgment-routed even when narrower process and follow-up facts are structured.

items.title: A current code-of-conduct acknowledgement set exists for the scoped workforce population.
items.rationale: Workforce conduct acknowledgement evidence remains documentary and owner-attested in the current corridor.

items.title: Refresh triggers and disciplinary pathways are current for the scoped workforce conduct program.
items.rationale: Conduct governance remains documentary and owner-attested in the current corridor.

items.title: The scoped workforce conduct model is adequate for the behavioural and security risks that matter.
items.rationale: Workforce conduct adequacy stays judgment-routed even when acknowledgement and governance facts are structured.

items.title: A current disciplinary policy covers the scoped workforce populations.
items.rationale: Disciplinary policy evidence remains documentary and owner-attested in the current corridor.

items.title: Disciplinary action records are current for the scoped breach-review window.
items.rationale: Disciplinary action records remain documentary and owner-attested in the current corridor.

items.title: The scoped disciplinary model is adequate to respond proportionately and consistently when relevant breaches occur.
items.rationale: Disciplinary adequacy stays judgment-routed even when narrower policy and action facts are structured.

items.title: A current internal-audit program and schedule exist for the scoped control surface.
items.rationale: Internal-audit program evidence remains documentary and owner-attested in the current corridor.

items.title: Findings and follow-up are current for the scoped internal-audit cycle.
items.rationale: Internal-audit execution evidence remains documentary and owner-attested in the current corridor.

items.title: The scoped internal-audit model is adequate to provide independent, useful assurance over the controls that matter.
items.rationale: Internal-audit adequacy stays judgment-routed even when narrower program and execution facts are structured.

items.title: A current clear-desk and clear-screen policy exists for the scoped work areas.
items.rationale: Workspace-hygiene policy evidence remains documentary and owner-attested in the current corridor.

items.title: Communication and coverage are current for the scoped clear-desk and clear-screen program.
items.rationale: Workspace-hygiene coverage remains documentary and owner-attested in the current corridor.

items.title: The scoped workspace hygiene model is adequate to reduce inadvertent physical disclosure and shoulder-surfing risks.
items.rationale: Workspace-hygiene adequacy stays judgment-routed even when narrower policy and coverage facts are structured.

items.title: Current participation exists in the scoped external security or special-interest groups.
items.rationale: External security-community participation remains documentary and owner-attested in the current corridor.

items.title: Ownership and dissemination are current for scoped external security inputs.
items.rationale: Security-community output handling remains documentary and owner-attested in the current corridor.

items.title: The scoped security-community participation model is adequate to turn relevant external insights into useful internal action.
items.rationale: Security-community participation adequacy stays judgment-routed even when narrower membership and dissemination facts are structured.

note: Synthetic persisted routing artifact for the OpenCompliance prove/attest/judgment split.

mixedControls.title: Production change control decomposes into machine policy checks plus signed governance evidence.
mixedControls.rationale: The current corridor can prove repository and CI guardrails, but wider change-review governance still arrives as signed human evidence rather than a theorem over system state.

subObligations.title: Default branch protections are enforced for the scoped production repository.
subObligations.rationale: Deterministic repository policy state over a narrow branch-protection predicate.

subObligations.title: CI workflow policy constrains deployment to reviewed workflows and protected refs.
subObligations.rationale: Deterministic delivery-policy state over a narrow CI workflow predicate.

subObligations.title: The production change-review policy is approved and declares an emergency change path for the current review window.
subObligations.rationale: The wider change-review governance layer is still documentary even when repo and CI guardrails are machine-checked.
